text:
PET: Whiskers
DATE: 2024-01-15
ID: 12345

INTAKE TYPE:
Pet surrender - owner moving overseas

BACKGROUND:
3-year-old orange tabby cat brought to shelter by owner. Previously indoor-only pet 
with complete vaccination history. Owner reports cat is generally healthy and well-behaved. 
Whiskers has been with the family since adoption as a kitten from this same shelter. 
No history of aggression or destructive behavior. Good with children and other cats, 
but no exposure to dogs.

PREVIOUS CARE HISTORY:
- Spayed at 6 months
- Up-to-date on all vaccines (rabies, FVRCP)
- Annual vet checkups (last visit 2 months ago)
- No chronic health issues

CURRENT DIET:
- Dry food: Hill's Science Diet Adult Indoor Cat
- Wet food: Fancy Feast (twice weekly as treat)
- Always has access to fresh water

BEHAVIORAL OBSERVATIONS:
General: Alert and friendly, responsive to humans
Activity Level: Moderate - enjoys playing with feather toys and laser pointers
Socialization: Seeks human attention, purrs readily when petted
Litter Box: Well-trained, uses covered litter box consistently, prefers unscented litter
Sleep Pattern: Typical cat behavior - sleeps 14-16 hours daily, most active at dawn/dusk
Vocalizations: Quiet, occasional meowing for food or attention

SHELTER NOTES:
Whiskers adjusted well to the shelter environment within 24 hours. Shows good appetite 
and normal grooming behavior. Friendly with staff and other cats in the viewing room. 
Would do well in a quiet home or with an experienced cat owner. May need gradual 
introduction to children or other pets.

MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: Temp 101.5F (normal), Weight 10.2 lbs (ideal)
Body Condition: Good, appropriate weight for frame
Coat: Clean and well-groomed, shiny orange tabby pattern
Eyes: Clear, no discharge
Ears: Clean, no mites or infection
Teeth: Minor tartar buildup, otherwise healthy
Heart/Lungs: Normal
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender

ASSESSMENT:
Healthy adult cat ready for adoption. Would thrive in calm household. Recommend 
continued annual vet visits and dental cleaning within 6 months. Owner expressed 
strong emotional attachment and asked to be contacted if issues arise.

ADOPTION STATUS: Available
RECOMMENDED HOME: Indoor only, adults or families with older children

PET: Max
DATE: 2024-01-16
ID: 67890

INTAKE TYPE:
Stray pickup - found by animal control officer

BACKGROUND:
2-year-old male Labrador Retriever mix found wandering near Main Street Park. No collar 
or microchip detected on initial scan. Dog appeared friendly but anxious. Animal control 
officer reports dog was limping slightly on right front leg when picked up. Has been 
housed in shelter for 72 hours; no owner has come forward to claim. Mandatory hold 
period will end in 4 days.

PREVIOUS HISTORY:
Unknown - appears to have been socialized and housetrained, suggesting prior home 
experience. Shows signs of basic training (responds to "sit" command).

CURRENT DIET:
- Purina Dog Chow (2 cups twice daily)
- Adequate water intake
- Good appetite noted

BEHAVIORAL OBSERVATIONS:
General: Energetic and playful, shows some anxiety in kennel
Activity Level: High - needs regular exercise and mental stimulation
Socialization: Very friendly with people, submissive body language
Dog Interaction: Mixed - playful with some dogs, reactive to larger dominant males
Training: Knows basic commands (sit, down), walks reasonably well on leash with pulling
Anxiety Indicators: Pacing in kennel, whining when alone, settles with human presence
Play Style: Fetch-motivated, loves tennis balls and rope toys

SHELTER NOTES:
Max has been a staff favorite due to his friendly personality. Shows no aggression 
toward handlers. Does pull on leash during walks due to excitement. Would benefit 
from continued training and an active family. May have separation anxiety based on 
vocalization patterns when left alone. Settles well with a Kong toy or bully stick.

MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: Temp 101.8F (normal), Weight 52 lbs (slightly underweight)
Body Condition: Thin but not emaciated, ribs slightly visible
Coat: Short brown coat with black markings, some matting and dirt noted
Eyes: Clear and bright
Ears: Clean, no infection
Teeth: Young adult dentition, mild tartar, no broken teeth
Musculoskeletal: Mild lameness right front leg, no swelling or obvious injury noted
Heart/Lungs: Normal
Skin: Minor scratches and abrasions consistent with outdoor living

VETERINARY TREATMENT:
- Neutering scheduled for tomorrow (required before adoption)
- Vaccinations administered: DAPP, Bordetella, Rabies
- Deworming treatment given
- Flea/tick prevention applied
- Right leg lameness - appears to be resolving, likely soft tissue strain
- Recommend recheck in 1 week if limping persists

ASSESSMENT:
Young adult Labrador mix with friendly temperament and high energy. Needs active home 
with yard and patient owner willing to continue training. Good candidate for first-time 
dog owner with time to dedicate to exercise needs. Separation anxiety may improve 
with routine and proper introduction to being alone.

ADOPTION STATUS: Available after neutering (2 days)
RECOMMENDED HOME: Active household, fenced yard preferred, older children okay

PET: Luna
DATE: 2024-01-17
ID: 11223

INTAKE TYPE:
Foster care return - long-term behavioral evaluation

BACKGROUND:
5-year-old female Australian Shepherd mix returning from 8-week foster placement. Luna 
was originally surrendered by owner due to "destructive behavior and excessive energy." 
Foster family reports significant improvement with structured routine and daily exercise. 
Dog was placed in experienced foster home specializing in high-energy breeds. Foster 
reports indicate Luna thrives with clear boundaries and mental stimulation activities.

PREVIOUS HISTORY:
- Adopted as puppy from shelter in 2020
- Returned to shelter in 2023 due to behavioral issues
- Had two previous homes before current surrender
- Owner-reported issues: chewing furniture, excessive barking, escaping from yard
- No formal training classes attended

FOSTER CARE REPORT:
Foster family provided detailed daily logs. Key findings:
- Destructive behavior significantly reduced with adequate exercise (60+ minutes daily)
- Chewing redirected to appropriate toys (Kongs, bully sticks, puzzle toys)
- Responds well to positive reinforcement training
- Learned "place" command and crate training
- No escape attempts when physical and mental needs met
- Occasional leash reactivity toward small dogs, manageable with redirection

CURRENT DIET:
- Blue Buffalo Large Breed (3 cups daily, split between meals)
- Training treats: small pieces of chicken, cheese
- No food allergies noted

BEHAVIORAL OBSERVATIONS:
General: High-energy working breed with strong herding instincts
Activity Level: Very high - requires minimum 2 hours exercise daily
Intelligence: Highly intelligent, learns quickly, needs mental challenges
Socialization: Good with dog-savvy people, wary of fast-moving children
Dog Interaction: Selective - good with calm dogs, may try to "herd" smaller dogs
Cat Compatible: Unknown - shows high prey drive toward squirrels
Training Progress: Knows sit, down, stay, place, leave it, drop it
Anxiety Indicators: Can become anxious if under-exercised, paces and whines
Best Activities: Hiking, fetch, agility-style obstacles, snuffle mat, puzzle toys

SHELTER OBSERVATION NOTES (POST-FOSTER):
Luna has maintained good behavior in shelter despite stressful environment. Staff 
notes she settles quickly after exercise. Shows clear preference for interactive 
play over solo activities. Herding instinct apparent when in play yard - tries to 
gather and manage other dogs. Would do best as only pet or with larger, calm dogs.

MEDICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: Temp 101.2F (normal), Weight 48 lbs (ideal for breed)
Body Condition: Excellent, well-muscled and lean
Coat: Thick blue merle coat with white markings, well-groomed
Eyes: Bright, one blue eye, one brown (common in breed)
Ears: Clean, no infection
Teeth: Excellent condition, adult dentition
Heart/Lungs: Normal
Musculoskeletal: Strong and athletic build
Spay Status: Spayed (prior to first surrender)

VETERINARY NOTES:
- All vaccinations current
- Monthly heartworm prevention
- No chronic health issues
- Recommend continued high-quality diet
- Joint supplements recommended for active breed aging prevention

ASSESSMENT:
Intelligent, high-energy Australian Shepherd mix who requires experienced owner 
committed to providing adequate physical exercise and mental stimulation. Not 
suitable for apartment living or sedentary households. With proper outlets for 
energy and clear structure, Luna is a loyal and well-behaved companion. Previous 
behavioral issues were management problems, not temperament flaws.

ADOPTION STATUS: Available for special placement
RECOMMENDED HOME: 
- Experienced dog owner familiar with herding breeds
- Active lifestyle (runners, hikers preferred)
- Fenced yard required
- Adults only or families with older children (12+)
- No cats or small pets
- Commitment to continued training and daily exercise
- Ideal for someone wanting a jogging/adventure partner

SPECIAL NOTES:
Adoption contract will require proof of adequate fencing and commitment to minimum 
exercise requirements. Follow-up home visit recommended at 30 days.
